post.description:
Mint the money-plane token pair for an already-identified user, keyed by their
 concierge user id. Service-to-service: authenticated by the shared issuance token
 in the `authorization` metadata (Bearer), never a user token. The user must already
 exist locally (provisioned by the cross-plane bridge) and not be frozen.

Publish the current signing public keys (JWKS). Polled by downstream services'
 `evbanking_auth` verifier to refresh its local cache; also consulted on an
 unknown-`kid` miss. Carries only public key material.

List the caller's active sessions (refresh-token families). Identity is proven by
 the presented refresh token (the BFF holds it server-side); the family that owns
 that token is flagged `current`.

Revoke the presented refresh token's family. With `revoke_all` set, also bumps
 the user's `token_version` so every outstanding access token is rejected hub-side.

Rotate a refresh token: returns a fresh access+refresh pair and invalidates
 the presented refresh token (rotation + reuse detection in the central store).

Revoke one of the caller's sessions by id (must belong to the same user). Revoking
 the current session is allowed (acts like a sign-out of this device).

Dispatch a queued withdrawal to custody once its rail has liquidity (the treasury
 worker). Stands in for an automated rebalance/top-up trigger. Idempotent.

Fail a queued fund redemption (operator): voids the reserved burn, returning the
 user's units. Idempotent.

Fail a processing user withdrawal that never reached the chain: voids the
 reservation, refunding the user. NEVER call this if the broadcast may have landed
 on-chain — that would double-pay. Idempotent.
Operator (TreasuryRead): the money-plane operations mode (the read-only kill-switch).

The treasury: per-rail on-chain liquidity (Layer 2) and the claims it backs
 (Layer 1), read live from TigerBeetle (Read-First).
Operator (TreasuryRead): outbox rows the relay parked (needs-intervention).

Operator (RedemptionSettle): the cross-user queue of redemptions awaiting settle,
 oldest first — the "Valuation & redemptions" screen's clear-the-queue surface.
 (Est. cash = units × current NAV is computed client-side from the posted NAV.)

Post a fund's total AUM; NAV is derived = AUM / units_outstanding (units read live
 from TigerBeetle). Rejects zero units and, unless `override` is set, a NAV move
 beyond the safety threshold vs the last mark (the fat-finger guard on the trust seam).

Record a settled on-chain deposit, crediting a party's claim. Idempotent by
 `tx_ref` — recording the same chain tx twice credits once.
Inject the company's own capital onto a rail (Dr WALLET / Cr FUND).

Admin (OperationsManage): toggle read-only mode. When on, every user money mutation
 (withdraw / subscribe / redeem) is rejected — "pause deposits & withdrawals".

Settle a queued fund redemption once the fund's claim is liquid (the treasury
 worker): prices at the settle-time NAV and pays the cash. Idempotent.

Settle a user withdrawal once it has the required on-chain confirmations: posts the
 reservation and moves the net out of custody. `tx_ref` is the chain transaction.
 Stands in for a chain watcher + custody confirmation callback. Idempotent.

Admin (OutboxManage): clear `parked_at` so the relay re-drives the event once the
 cause is fixed; refused for a compensated row — its recovery event already
 applied, so re-driving would double-apply.
Cancel a still-queued redemption (returns the reserved units).
The current NAV (price per share) for a fund, plus freshness.
The caller's position in one fund (units, NAV, value, cost basis, P&L).
All of the caller's fund positions.
The caller's redemptions for the fund surface (newest first).
Redeem `units` of a fund back to cash (accept-and-queue; settle-time priced).

Subscribe `amount` of free balance into a fund, minting units at the current NAV.
 Returns the mint receipt; the holding updates once the relay applies it.

Admin (allowlist): disable a user, freezing future sign-in/refresh without
 deleting the record.

The calling user's live balance, read straight from TigerBeetle (Read-First).
 No Postgres-stored amount exists; the response is authoritative as of `as_of`.
The calling user's own identity (resolved from the access-token `sub`).

Admin (UserBalanceRead): read ANY user's live balance by id — the operator
 console's user-detail drawer. The self-service GetBalance above acts on the
 caller's own `sub`; this one takes an explicit target and is role-gated.

Admin (allowlist): bump a user's token_version so all their outstanding access
 tokens are rejected hub-side. The token-revocation control surface.

Update the caller's own editable profile fields (full-replace of the set in
 UpdateProfileRequest). Identity/auth fields (email, status) are not editable here.

Cancel one of the caller's still-queued withdrawals (refund). Rejected once the
 withdrawal is processing (a broadcast may have landed).
The caller's deposit address on a network (stable; derived once and reused).

The caller's wallet — the unified lifecycle balance, deposit addresses, and the
 per-rail withdrawable view.
The caller's confirmed on-chain deposits, newest first.
The caller's withdrawals, newest first.

Request an on-chain withdrawal of free balance to an external address. Accepted
 (and reserved) as long as the unified balance covers it; it ships immediately when
 the chosen rail is liquid, otherwise it is queued until the treasury tops it up.

Exchange a Google authorization code (with its PKCE verifier) for concierge's
 OWN tokens. Called by the cabinet BFF on the OAuth callback. Provisions the
 user on first sign-in in the directory module.

Publish the current signing public keys (JWKS). Polled by downstream services'
 `evconcierge_auth` verifier to refresh its local cache; also consulted on an
 unknown-`kid` miss. Carries only public key material.

List the caller's active sessions (refresh-token families). Identity is proven by
 the presented refresh token (the BFF holds it server-side); the family that owns
 that token is flagged `current`.

Revoke the presented refresh token's family. With `revoke_all` set, also bumps
 the user's `token_version` so every outstanding access token is rejected.

Rotate a refresh token: returns a fresh access+refresh pair and invalidates
 the presented refresh token (rotation + reuse detection in the central store).

Revoke one of the caller's sessions by id (must belong to the same user). Revoking
 the current session is allowed (acts like a sign-out of this device).

Admin (allowlist): disable a user, freezing future sign-in/refresh without
 deleting the record. Emits SUSPENDED across the cross-plane bridge.
The calling user's own identity (resolved from the access-token `sub`).

Admin (UserRead): the full profile of ANY user by id (the admin sibling of the
 self-only GetMe), for the user-detail drawer.

Admin (UserRead): list/search users for the operator console — paginated, with
 optional role/KYC/status filters. Returns lightweight summaries, not full profiles.

Admin (allowlist): re-enable a disabled user, lifting the sign-in/refresh
 freeze. Emits REINSTATED across the cross-plane bridge.

Admin (allowlist): bump a user's token_version so all their outstanding access
 tokens are rejected. The token-revocation control surface.

Admin (allowlist): set a user's KYC level. Emits KYC_CHANGED across the
 cross-plane bridge (so the money plane learns the new level). Echoes the
 level now in effect.

Admin (RoleGrant, Owner only): set a user's platform access role. Emits
 ROLE_CHANGED across the cross-plane bridge so the money plane mirrors it. Echoes
 the role now in effect.

Update the caller's own editable profile fields (full-replace of the set in
 UpdateProfileRequest). Identity/auth fields (email, status) are not editable here.

user_id.title: user_id
user_id.description:
The target: the CONCIERGE user id (the handle the operator console carries from
 the identity plane's ListUsers) or the banking user id. Resolution is
 concierge-first via the bridge mirror (`users.concierge_user_id`), falling back
 to the banking id (existing money-plane callers stay valid); an id matching
 neither returns NOT_FOUND — never an authoritative zero.

banking.v1.Balance.title: Balance
banking.v1.Balance.description:
The user's single, network-agnostic balance, segmented by lifecycle. Every figure is
 a TigerBeetle-authoritative decimal USDT string; total = available + invested +
 pending_withdrawal.

created_at.title: created_at
created_at.description: unix seconds the hub recorded the credit

banking.v1.Deposit.title: Deposit
banking.v1.Deposit.description: A confirmed on-chain deposit credited to the caller's unified balance.

min_confirmations.title: min_confirmations
min_confirmations.description: confirmations before a deposit is credited

banking.v1.DepositScanCursor.title: DepositScanCursor
banking.v1.DepositScanCursor.description:
Seconds since a rail's deposit watcher last advanced/touched its per-network scan
 cursor (refreshed only by a completed scan cycle, so age ≈ time since the last
 healthy cycle; thresholds are the consumer's concern).

aum.title: aum
aum.description: last posted AUM (decimal USDT; empty if seed)

posted_at.title: posted_at
posted_at.description: unix seconds of the mark (0 = seed)

banking.v1.FundNav.title: FundNav
banking.v1.FundNav.description:
The current price of a fund's share, derived (AUM / units_outstanding) and frozen
 between operator marks. `posted_at` is 0 when the fund has never been marked (seed
 NAV 1.0); `stale` is true when the latest mark is older than the dealing window.

concierge_user_id.title: concierge_user_id
concierge_user_id.description:
The concierge canonical user id (a UUID) of the already-authenticated user. Banking
 maps it to its local mirror row (provisioned by the cross-plane bridge) and mints the
 money-plane pair for that user. Banking never sees Google's `sub` — the bridge carries
 it; the BFF carries this concierge id, which it has from the concierge sign-in.

user_agent.title: user_agent
user_agent.description:
Device metadata captured by the BFF at sign-in, stored on the refresh-token family
 for the "sessions & devices" surface. Best-effort, free-form.

banking.v1.Jwk.title: Jwk
banking.v1.Jwk.description:
One JSON Web Key. Ed25519 keys are OKP: kty="OKP", crv="Ed25519", x=base64url
 public key, alg="EdDSA". `kid` is a stable thumbprint of the public key.

refresh_token.title: refresh_token
refresh_token.description: The caller's current refresh token: proves identity and flags the current session.

revoke_all.title: revoke_all
revoke_all.description:
When true, bump the user's token_version (coarse "revoke all"), not just this
 refresh family.

withdrawable.title: withdrawable
withdrawable.description: max requestable on this rail (= available)

withdrawal_fee.title: withdrawal_fee
withdrawal_fee.description: flat network fee retained on a withdrawal

banking.v1.NetworkWithdrawable.title: NetworkWithdrawable
banking.v1.NetworkWithdrawable.description:
Per-rail withdrawal options (the accept-and-queue UX). `withdrawable` is the whole
 available balance (a request beyond `instant` is accepted and queued); `instant` is
 the portion that ships without queueing = min(available, rail liquidity).

banking.v1.ParkedEvent.title: ParkedEvent
banking.v1.ParkedEvent.description:
One parked outbox row. `reason` is the relay's last error; `parked_at` is unix
 seconds; `compensated` marks a half-applied event whose recovery already ran
 (never unparkable).

cost_basis.title: cost_basis
cost_basis.description: net cash invested, average cost (decimal USDT)

nav_as_of.title: nav_as_of
nav_as_of.description: unix seconds of the NAV mark (0 = seed NAV)

banking.v1.Position.title: Position
banking.v1.Position.description:
A user's holding in one fund. `value = units × nav`; `pnl = value − cost_basis`
 (a signed decimal — losses are negative). `nav_as_of` is the unix-seconds timestamp
 of the NAV mark used (0 when the fund is on the bootstrap seed NAV).

aum.title: aum
aum.description: decimal USDT — the fund's total assets under management

override.title: override
override.description: bypass the NAV-move safety guard (operator confirmed)

custody.title: custody
custody.description: liquid on-chain USDT held in this rail's custody wallet

treasury_address.title: treasury_address
treasury_address.description: the rail's hot wallet — fund USDT (liquidity) + gas here

onchain_usdt.title: onchain_usdt
onchain_usdt.description: decimal USDT actually on-chain in the treasury wallet

onchain_gas.title: onchain_gas
onchain_gas.description: decimal native units (BNB | TRX | TON) for gas

banking.v1.RailLiquidity.title: RailLiquidity
banking.v1.RailLiquidity.description:
Per-rail on-chain liquidity (the treasury / Layer 2). The `treasury_*`/`onchain_*`
 fields are the operator funding view, read best-effort from the chain — empty means
 the rail is unconfigured or the read was unavailable, never an error.

scan_cursors.title: scan_cursors
scan_cursors.description:
Per-rail deposit-scan diagnostics; only configured rails appear. A large age on
 a configured rail means the deposit scan is stalled — deposits confirm on-chain
 but are not credited. Diagnostics only: it does NOT gate `ready`.

banking.v1.ReadinessResponse.title: ReadinessResponse
banking.v1.ReadinessResponse.description:
`ready` is the gate the orchestrator acts on; the rest are diagnostics so a
 not-ready response says *why* without scraping logs. `parked_rows` are outbox
 events the relay could not apply (need operator intervention); `backlog` is the
 count of undispatched rows and the age of the oldest, surfacing a wedged relay.

tx_ref.title: tx_ref
tx_ref.description: the on-chain tx reference — the idempotency key

network.title: network
network.description: bep20 | trc20 | ton — the rail it arrived on

party_kind.title: party_kind
party_kind.description:
The party credited. `party_kind` ∈ {piggybank, user, service}; `party_id` is the
 user UUID or service id (empty for piggybank, which credits the fund's capital).

recorded.title: recorded
recorded.description: false if `tx_ref` was already recorded (idempotent no-op)

banking.v1.Redemption.title: Redemption
banking.v1.Redemption.description:
A redemption in the accept-and-queue saga. `units` are fixed at request; `nav`/`cash`
 are empty until settle (settle-time pricing). State ∈ {queued, completed, failed,
 cancelled}.

banking.v1.RedemptionQueueItem.title: RedemptionQueueItem
banking.v1.RedemptionQueueItem.description:
One queued redemption for the operator queue. `email` is the mirrored identity email
 (may be empty if the bridge hasn't populated it). `created_at` is unix seconds.

network.title: network
network.description: bep20 | trc20 | ton — the rail to ship on

amount.title: amount
amount.description: gross decimal USDT to debit (the fee is taken from this)

refresh_token.title: refresh_token
refresh_token.description: The caller's current refresh token (proves identity).

session_id.title: session_id
session_id.description: The session id to revoke (must belong to the same user).

network.title: network
network.description: bep20 | trc20 | ton — the rail the capital lands on

current.title: current
current.description: True for the family that owns the refresh token presented on the listing call.

banking.v1.Session.title: Session
banking.v1.Session.description: One active session = one refresh-token family. Timestamps are unix SECONDS.

banking.v1.Subscription.title: Subscription
banking.v1.Subscription.description:
The receipt for one subscription (mint): `cash` of the user's balance bought `units`
 at `nav`. The position itself is read via GetPosition once the relay has applied it.

banking.v1.TokenResponse.title: TokenResponse
banking.v1.TokenResponse.description:
A first-party token pair plus a snapshot of the principal. All expiries are
 unix SECONDS (matching the JWT `exp` claim); the BFF parses them with
 proto-loader `longs: String`.

bank.title: bank
bank.description: mocked USD liquidity (separate ledger; off-ramp FX)

total_custody.title: total_custody
total_custody.description: sum of rail custody — the asset side

reserved_for_withdrawals.title: reserved_for_withdrawals
reserved_for_withdrawals.description: of held_for_clients, locked by in-flight withdrawals

banking.v1.Treasury.title: Treasury
banking.v1.Treasury.description:
The treasury picture: per-rail liquidity (Layer 2) and the claims it backs (Layer 1).
 Under the unified-claim model the invariant is GLOBAL — `total_custody` (the asset
 side) equals the sum of all claims — so client liabilities are the remainder beyond
 the fund's own capital and retained fees.

banking.v1.UpdateProfileRequest.title: UpdateProfileRequest
banking.v1.UpdateProfileRequest.description:
The caller's editable profile, full-replace. Same 10 fields as the editable part of
 UserProfile; an empty string clears the field.

banking.v1.UserBalanceResponse.title: UserBalanceResponse
banking.v1.UserBalanceResponse.description:
A live ledger balance for a user. `amount` and `pending` are u128 minor-unit
 decimal STRINGS (proto3 has no u128; a string is lossless and matches
 proto-loader's `longs: String`). An investor account is credit-normal, so
 `amount` = posted credits − posted debits is non-negative by invariant.
 `authoritative` is true when read directly from TigerBeetle; `as_of` is unix
 seconds. The `authoritative`/`as_of` pair is the forward-compat seam for a future
 read projection — a projection may later return `authoritative=false` rows with
 no breaking proto change. (Named for the users surface; the company-money
 `BalanceService` owns its own balance messages.)

legal_name.title: legal_name
legal_name.description:
Editable profile fields (control plane; empty until the user sets them). email and
 status above stay read-only — email is the IdP's, status is admin-managed.

kyc_level.title: kyc_level
kyc_level.description:
Kept wire-identical with concierge.v1.UserProfile (the cabinet TS is aliased to the
 concierge shape). The identity plane OWNS kyc/role; the money plane mirrors them for
 gating but does not re-serve them on its self-service GetMe, so these stay default
 here — the fields exist for cross-plane wire parity.

user_id.title: user_id
user_id.description: The hub's canonical user id (a UUID) — this is the JWT `sub`, never Google's sub.

role.title: role
role.description:
Kept wire-identical with concierge.v1.UserSummary (the cabinet TS is aliased to the
 concierge shape). The money plane doesn't serve the role here — the cabinet reads it
 from concierge's login summary — so this stays empty; the field exists for parity.

banking.v1.UserSummary.title: UserSummary
banking.v1.UserSummary.description:
Minimal principal snapshot returned with a freshly issued token pair, so the BFF
 can populate a session without a second round trip.

deposit_addresses.title: deposit_addresses
deposit_addresses.description: a rail to top up on (per network)

amount.title: amount
amount.description: gross debited from the user (decimal USDT)

net_amount.title: net_amount
net_amount.description: amount − fee, the amount sent on-chain

banking.v1.Withdrawal.title: Withdrawal
banking.v1.Withdrawal.description:
A user withdrawal aggregate, projected. `state` advances queued → processing →
 completed (settled on confirmations) | failed (voided, refunded); a queued one may
 also be cancelled (voided, refunded).

created_at.title: created_at
created_at.description: Unix SECONDS the user was provisioned (joined).

concierge.v1.AdminUserSummary.title: AdminUserSummary
concierge.v1.AdminUserSummary.description:
A lightweight user row for the operator console's user list (not the full profile).
 Named distinctly from auth.proto's login `UserSummary` (same package).

auth_code.title: auth_code
auth_code.description: The Google authorization code from the OAuth redirect.

code_verifier.title: code_verifier
code_verifier.description: The PKCE code verifier the BFF generated for this transaction (S256).

redirect_uri.title: redirect_uri
redirect_uri.description: The exact redirect_uri used in the authorize request (Google requires a match).

nonce.title: nonce
nonce.description: The nonce the BFF put in the authorize request; matched against Google's id_token.

user_agent.title: user_agent
user_agent.description:
Device metadata captured by the BFF at sign-in, stored on the refresh-token family
 for the "sessions & devices" surface. Best-effort, free-form.

concierge.v1.Jwk.title: Jwk
concierge.v1.Jwk.description:
One JSON Web Key. Ed25519 keys are OKP: kty="OKP", crv="Ed25519", x=base64url
 public key, alg="EdDSA". `kid` is a stable thumbprint of the public key.

refresh_token.title: refresh_token
refresh_token.description: The caller's current refresh token: proves identity and flags the current session.

query.title: query
query.description: Free-text match over email/user_id (empty = no text filter).

limit.title: limit
limit.description: Page window. `limit` is clamped server-side; `offset` is a simple row offset.

total.title: total
total.description: Total matching the filters (before the page window), for the list header count.

revoke_all.title: revoke_all
revoke_all.description:
When true, bump the user's token_version (coarse "revoke all"), not just this
 refresh family.

refresh_token.title: refresh_token
refresh_token.description: The caller's current refresh token (proves identity).

session_id.title: session_id
session_id.description: The session id to revoke (must belong to the same user).

current.title: current
current.description: True for the family that owns the refresh token presented on the listing call.

concierge.v1.Session.title: Session
concierge.v1.Session.description: One active session = one refresh-token family. Timestamps are unix SECONDS.

kyc_level.title: kyc_level
kyc_level.description: The user's KYC level now in effect (echoes the applied level).

role.title: role
role.description: The role now in effect (echoes the applied role).

concierge.v1.TokenResponse.title: TokenResponse
concierge.v1.TokenResponse.description:
A first-party token pair plus a snapshot of the principal. All expiries are
 unix SECONDS (matching the JWT `exp` claim); the BFF parses them with
 proto-loader `longs: String`.

concierge.v1.UpdateProfileRequest.title: UpdateProfileRequest
concierge.v1.UpdateProfileRequest.description:
The caller's editable profile, full-replace. Same 10 fields as the editable part of
 UserProfile; an empty string clears the field.

legal_name.title: legal_name
legal_name.description:
Editable profile fields (control plane; empty until the user sets them). email and
 status above stay read-only — email is the IdP's, status is admin-managed.

kyc_level.title: kyc_level
kyc_level.description: The user's KYC level (admin-managed; mirrored to the money plane).

role.title: role
role.description:
The user's platform access role (snake_case: investor/operator/admin/owner).
 Admin-managed via SetRole; read-only here.

user_id.title: user_id
user_id.description: The concierge canonical user id (a UUID) — this is the JWT `sub`, never Google's sub.

role.title: role
role.description:
The user's platform access role (snake_case: investor/operator/admin/owner), so the
 BFF can gate the admin console at sign-in without a second round trip. Read-only.

concierge.v1.UserSummary.title: UserSummary
concierge.v1.UserSummary.description:
Minimal principal snapshot returned with a freshly issued token pair, so the BFF
 can populate a session without a second round trip.

connect-protocol-version.title: Connect-Protocol-Version
connect-protocol-version.description: Define the version of the Connect protocol

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of [google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the [google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized by the client.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is no limit on the number of messages.

type.description: A URL that acts as a globally unique identifier for the type of the serialized message. For example: `type.googleapis.com/google.rpc.ErrorInfo`. This is used to determine the schema of the data in the `value` field and is the discriminator for the `debug` field.

value.description: The Protobuf message, serialized as bytes and base64-encoded. The specific message type is identified by the `type` field.

debug.title: Debug
debug.description: Deserialized error detail payload. The 'type' field indicates the schema. This field is for easier debugging and should not be relied upon for application logic.

connect.error_details.Any.description: Contains an arbitrary serialized message along with a @type that describes the type of the serialized message, with an additional debug field for ConnectRPC error details.

tags.name: banking.v1.AuthService
tags.description:
AuthService — the money plane's token issuance and authorization surface.

 This is the MONEY plane: it does NOT do third-party (Google) OAuth — identity and
 sign-in live wholly in the concierge plane. Users are mirrored here by the one-way
 cross-plane bridge (concierge `CREATED` → a local row keyed by `auth_subject`).
 This service only issues/rotates the hub's OWN first-party asymmetric JWTs
 (EdDSA/Ed25519, `aud=banking-core`) for those already-identified users, and
 publishes verification keys. Services do NOT call this per request to authorize:
 they verify access tokens locally against the cached JWKS via the shared
 `evbanking_auth` flow (no round trip, no per-service token storage). They DO call
 `Jwks` periodically to refresh that cache, since the hub speaks only gRPC (no HTTP
 `.well-known` endpoint).

 `IssueUserToken` is the concierge→banking token-exchange seam: after the cabinet
 BFF authenticates a user against concierge, it calls this server-to-server (with the
 shared bridge issuance token, NOT a user token) to mint the money-plane pair for that
 user. Banking trusts its OWN service credential, never concierge's issuer — so a
 leaked concierge token can't move money. See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

tags.name: banking.v1.FundsService
tags.description:
FundsService — a user's investment in funds via the service currency (units/shares).

 Subscribing buys units at the current NAV; a holding's value is units × NAV, so
 profit comes from NAV rising, not from extra units. Redeeming is accept-and-queue:
 units are reserved immediately and the cash is priced + paid at settle (settle-time
 NAV). Every amount/unit/price is a decimal STRING (proto3 has no u128; a string is
 lossless and matches proto-loader's `longs: String`). Self-service RPCs act on the
 caller's own access-token `sub`. See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

tags.name: banking.v1.BalanceService
tags.description:
BalanceService — the bank's treasury and company capital (operator surface).

 Reads authoritative balances from the TigerBeetle ledger; metadata and events from
 Postgres. Every amount is a decimal USDT STRING (proto3 has no u128; a string is
 lossless and matches proto-loader's `longs: String`). All RPCs are admin-gated
 (allowlist) at the boundary. See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

tags.name: banking.v1.HealthService
tags.description:
Health surface for the hub's gRPC service plane.

   Check     — liveness: is the process up? Trivial, unauthenticated; backs the
               `cabinet` BFF smoke path (browser -> BFF -> gRPC) and service checks.
   Readiness — can this instance actually move money? Pings Postgres + TigerBeetle
               and inspects the outbox (parked rows / dispatch backlog). An
               orchestrator/LB drains an instance that is up but not ready instead of
               routing money-moving traffic to it.

tags.name: banking.v1.UsersService
tags.description:
UsersService — investor accounts and their investments.

 Identity metadata is the Postgres control plane; money is read live from the
 authoritative TigerBeetle ledger and is NEVER re-bookkept in Postgres. Every
 RPC is authorized from the verified access-token Claims injected by core's
 inbound auth layer; self-service RPCs act on the caller's own `sub`, admin RPCs
 are gated by the hub's admin allowlist (config). See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

tags.name: banking.v1.WalletService
tags.description:
WalletService — a user's own crypto wallet: one unified USDT balance, deposit
 addresses, and withdrawals. Networks (BEP20/TRC20/TON) are a transaction attribute
 (where you deposit / where a withdrawal ships), never a balance dimension — the user
 holds a single fungible USDT balance, segmented by lifecycle.

 Every RPC acts on the caller's own access-token `sub`. Balances are read live from the
 authoritative TigerBeetle ledger (Read-First); amounts are decimal USDT STRINGS
 (proto3 has no u128; a string is lossless and matches proto-loader's `longs: String`).
 A withdrawal is a queued saga (reserve → dispatch → settle/void): it is accepted even
 when the chosen rail is short on liquidity and queued until the treasury tops it up.
 The operator-driven dispatch/settle/fail RPCs live on BalanceService; the custody
 broadcast and deposit-address derivation sit behind ports (a stub in this scaffold).
 See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md and piggybank/core/PATTERNS.md.

tags.name: concierge.v1.UserDirectory
tags.description:
UserDirectory — the authoritative user/identity control plane.

 Owns investor accounts and their profile metadata. This is the identity plane:
 money lives in the separate banking plane and is NEVER mirrored here. Every RPC
 is authorized from the verified access-token Claims injected by the inbound auth
 layer; self-service RPCs act on the caller's own `sub`, admin RPCs are gated by
 the admin allowlist (config).

tags.name: concierge.v1.AuthService
tags.description:
AuthService — the concierge plane's token issuance and authorization surface.

 Issues first-party asymmetric JWTs (EdDSA/Ed25519) after Google OAuth2 sign-in,
 rotates refresh tokens, and publishes verification keys. Services do NOT call
 this per request to authorize: they verify access tokens locally against the
 cached JWKS via the shared `evconcierge_auth` flow (no round trip, no
 per-service token storage). They DO call `Jwks` periodically to refresh that
 cache, since concierge speaks only gRPC (no HTTP `.well-known` endpoint).

 The cabinet BFF is the OAuth confidential client. It runs the browser-facing
 authorize redirect (PKCE + state + nonce) and then calls `Exchange`
 server-to-server with the Google authorization code; the auth module performs
 the confidential code→token exchange with Google, validates Google's id_token,
 and discards it — Google's token is never forwarded inward.
